summary: Backend-only testing for FoodPro POS. Ran 16 pytest cases against the public preview URL covering auth, subscription, currencies, exchange-rates (Frankfurter + open.er-api fallback), products, orders, QR, discounts, invoice settings, master-password, WebSocket and service-worker. 13 passed, 2 skipped (no demo products; spec endpoint name mismatch), 1 failed (USD exchange rates do not include SAR — real bug). Also identified one infra/routing concern with WebSocket /ws not reachable via the public ingress.

critical.issue: Response does not contain SAR. refreshRates('USD') hits Frankfurter (30 currencies, no SAR) and never merges with open.er-api.com fallback because the fallback path only runs when Frankfurter returns empty. Result: USD-based rates missing major non-Frankfurter currencies (SAR, AED, KWD, BHD, QAR, OMR, EGP, JOD, etc.). Review request explicitly required SAR to be present.

minor.issue: Returns 404. Actual mounted route is GET /api/discount-settings (returns {settings: []} = 200). Either rename the route or add an alias. Spec/route inconsistency.

Backend handshake works locally (HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols on localhost:8001/ws and frame {type:connected} received). Via public URL /ws is proxied to the frontend (returns text/html 200). Kubernetes ingress only forwards /api/* to the backend. Either mount socket-broker at /api/ws or extend ingress to forward /ws upgrades. Otherwise PWA clients on the public domain cannot use realtime.

action_items: Fix refreshRates() in /app/artifacts/api-server/src/routes/exchange-rates.ts to merge Frankfurter + open.er-api.com results (Union, not fallback-only) so that USD base contains SAR and other middle-east currencies. Then rebuild and restart backend.
Align discount settings route: either rename to /api/discounts/settings or add an alias router.get('/discounts/settings') so the spec matches.
Expose WebSocket /ws via the public ingress (mount at /api/ws or update ingress rules) so PWA/realtime works for end-users, not only on localhost.
critical_code_review_comments: refreshRates() uses fallback-only semantics; should merge providers so the union of supported currencies is persisted.
Route naming is inconsistent: discounts logic lives at /api/discount-settings while the rest of the resource uses pluralised paths (/api/qr-orders, /api/invoice-settings). Prefer one convention.
Socket broker is mounted at /ws (root) which is unreachable through the standard /api/* ingress; route it under /api or update ingress.
exchange-rates.ts top-level void refreshRates() calls run on import — fine, but consider awaiting in a startup hook to avoid race with first GET on cold start.
test_credentials: demo@foodpro.com / Demo2026! (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md) — login returns JWT, tenant=foodpro-demo, plan=enterprise/active confirmed.
seed_data_creation: None. /api/products returned empty for demo tenant so order create/complete test was skipped. Recommend seeding at least one demo product so the order flow can be exercised.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file at /app/backend/tests/test_foodpro_backend.py covers all listed endpoints. Re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_foodpro_backend.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/foodpro_results.xml. The only real backend bug is missing SAR in USD exchange rates — re-test test_rates_usd_includes_sar after refreshRates is fixed. Skip on order test will auto-resolve once a demo product is seeded. WS test currently passes via localhost fallback; once /ws is exposed publicly, simplify to only test the public URL.
rca of the issue:
Bug: GET /api/exchange-rates?base=USD missing SAR.
Reproduction: 1) POST /api/auth/login (demo creds) → token. 2) POST /api/exchange-rates/refresh {base:'USD'} → {ok:true}. 3) GET /api/exchange-rates?base=USD → rates contains 30 target_currency rows from Frankfurter only; SAR absent.
Root cause: In /app/artifacts/api-server/src/routes/exchange-rates.ts:17-39, refreshRates() assigns `rates` from Frankfurter first; the open.er-api.com block runs only when `rates` is still null. For USD, Frankfurter returns 30 currencies (success), so the fallback never executes and SAR/AED/KWD/etc. are never written to the exchange_rates table.
Mitigation: Change the logic to merge both providers (call both, then `rates = { ...erApiRates, ...frankfurterRates }`) so the union covers all 160+ currencies including SAR. Alternatively, after Frankfurter succeeds, still fetch open.er-api.com and INSERT/ON CONFLICT upsert any currencies Frankfurter doesn't have.

Secondary issue: Discount settings spec mismatch — route registered as /api/discount-settings, spec expects /api/discounts/settings. Add an alias or rename.

Tertiary: /ws not routed through public ingress; works on localhost:8001 only.
